title: Oncology Cycle Visit Alignment
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: oncology cycle visit alignment.
assumptions: Appointment slots and time blocks are mutually exclusive resources and consumed exclusively when reserved.
Resource availability is deterministic for the planning horizon (no probabilistic availability modelling).
A single cycle visit maps to one primary appointment slot and may consume multiple ancillary resources.
Cross-unit time alignment uses discrete time blocks that can be reserved to synchronize windows between units.
risks: Resource conflicts due to concurrent reservations across units leading to schedule collisions.
Patient no-shows or late arrivals that invalidate reserved time blocks and cascade to downstream allocations.
Equipment failure or medication batch shortages after allocation, requiring manual re-planning.
Insufficient coordination between teams (care_unit mismatch) leading to missed confirmations and delayed treatments.
Timing misalignment between units when cross-unit alignment action is not executed or fails.
